notes: Brand-stated drop is 5mm but lab measured 9.6mm. Toebox narrow/tapered (70.1mm). Toebox durability 2/5, heel padding durability rated 'Bad'. Outsole durability 'Good'. Midsole softness 39.5 AC (balanced). Flexibility 13.7N. Creel Jacquard Knit upper. Early-Stage Meta Rocker. Supercritical EVA, not PEBA.

Lab-measured values used (brand claims differ: 41.5mm stack, 8mm drop, 8.5% energy return). Forefoot energy return 66.5%, forefoot stack measured separately. Midsole softness 30.3 AC (plush). Traction 0.53 wet-grip. Flexibility 11.2N. Toebox/outsole durability rated qualitatively: toebox 'Bad', outsole 'Good' (not numeric). Heel counter stiffness 'Moderate', torsional rigidity 'Stiff' - no numeric scores given. No plate; subtle forefoot rocker. Jacquard mesh upper.

Drop is lab-measured (11.4mm) vs 10mm claimed. Heel energy return 55.5%, forefoot 61.4%. Air Zoom units in heel and forefoot. Midsole softness 35.2 AC. Toebox width 72.9mm (tapered). Low grip score (0.25), poor wet traction. Flexible (9.4N). True to size. Worse than v40 in cold. Heel counter rated 'Stiff', torsional rigidity 'Flexible' but not given as 1-5 numbers.

Winged nylon plate. Forefoot energy return 73.4%. Midsole softness 38.9 AC (firmer than v3). Slightly small fit, narrow tapered toebox (69.9mm). Neutral shoe. Flexibility 13.5N. Forefoot midsole width 114.7mm, heel 93.4mm. Drop listed as brand 8.0mm, lab 8.7mm.

Neutral daily trainer with mild stability features (reinforced sidewalls, stiff heel counter). Forefoot energy return 62.7%. Midsole softness 41.5 AC (firmer than average). Flexibility 9.6N. Toebox width 72.1mm, height 26.8mm. Fits true to size. Outsole durability rated 'Good' but no numeric durability score given. Cons: non-gusseted tongue, stiff heel counter, low toebox volume.

Lab-measured drop 6.1mm vs brand-stated 5mm. Shock absorption 127 SA. Midsole firm at 40.5 AC. Narrow fit (92.2mm width, 68.9mm toebox). Outsole hardness 76.0 HC, thickness 3.7mm. Stiffness 17.7N. Mild stability suitable for mild overpronation but no motion control features. Meta-rocker design.

PureGEL technology in heel for softer landings. Knit upper, expanded toebox. Midsole softness 39.5 AC (moderate-to-firm). Forefoot stack measured 32mm. Brand weight 304g, brand drop 8mm. Cons: heavy, limited energy return, unpadded tongue. Outsole uses AHAR+ and ASICSGRIP rubber. Surprisingly stable despite high stack. New version: Gel Nimbus 28.

Brand drop 8mm, lab 8.5mm. Midsole softness 19.0 HA. Outsole wear only 0.3mm (best-in-class durability). Tapered toebox (71.4mm) but tall (29.1mm). Wide forefoot midsole (125.1mm). Heel padding durability 4/5, toebox durability 3/5. Subpar breathability.

Neutral daily trainer with new PEBA-based Dreamstrike+ foam; uses EVA 'Support Rods' (not a true plate). No Continental rubber—Adiwear forefoot/heel plus translucent midfoot rubber. Midsole softness 35.0 AC; secondary foam 50.0 AC. Brand-stated drop 10mm, lab 9.7mm; brand stack heel 36mm/forefoot 26mm. Fits slightly small. Rockered geometry, best for heel strikers.

Pebax Wave Plate stops in midfoot (not full-length), so plate_material is Pebax but not carbon/nylon. Midsole softness 23.3 HA. Outsole durability excellent (0.6mm wear, ~500+ miles). Toebox durability scored 2/5, heel padding 5/5. Forefoot traction borderline on wet. True to size. Designed for heel strikers.

Dual-foam midsole: Nitro supercritical foam on top, firmer EVA carrier foam below (36.0 HA). Narrow toebox (70.4 mm) and forefoot. Full-length PumaGrip outsole. Midsole softness 35.8 AC, shock absorption 127 SA, stiffness 14.8N. True to size. Neutral daily trainer; brand drop spec 10mm vs lab 9.3mm.

Brand-stated heel 39mm, forefoot 35mm, drop 4mm vs lab measurements differ. Maximalist daily trainer; narrow midfoot and toebox (92.2mm width, 69.2mm toebox). Heavy at 311g. Outsole hardness 74 HC, outsole thickness 3mm, estimated 300+ miles durability. Midsole softness 41.5 AC. Wide platform (forefoot 126.5mm, heel 100.9mm). Has rocker, semi-gusseted tongue, finger-loop heel tab. Brand release date Jul 15, 2022.

Supercritical nitrogen-infused EVA (DNA Loft v3). Forefoot energy return 58.3%. Midsole softness 20.4 HA (balanced). Knit upper runs warm, not for hot summer runs. Tapered toebox. Rigid heel counter favors heel strikers. Neutral max-cushion daily trainer; GTS version available for stability. Shock absorption 133 SA heel. Flexibility 12.3N.

Maximalist neutral daily trainer. Brand stack 41.5/33.5mm but lab-measured 38/30.2mm. Lab drop 7.8mm (brand 8mm). Soft midsole 18.6 HA. Flexibility 13.2N. Forefoot midsole width 119.4mm, heel 98.9mm. Narrow fit (95.6mm). Shock absorption 133 SA heel/118 SA forefoot. Lackluster breathability and energy return. Heavier weight despite minimal outsole rubber. Suitable for mild pronators despite no medial post.

Full-length PWRRUN PB midsole upgrade. Brand drop 10mm, lab drop 9.7mm. Brand weight 286g matches lab. Neutral daily trainer with some non-intrusive stability features (sidewalls, broad platform). Shock absorption 140 SA, midsole softness 41.7 AC. Outsole durability rated 'Good' qualitatively. Wide width available in select markets. Standard width measured 97.5mm. Best for heel strikers and long easy/moderate runs; falls short at faster paces.

Maximalist stability shoe with PureGEL at heel, 4D Guidance System, non-rockered. Brand drop advertised 10mm vs measured 11.5mm. Midsole softness 35.3 AC. Outsole durability rated 'Decent'. Stability via sidewalls/chassis rather than traditional medial post; has_medial_post not explicitly stated. True to size.

Stability daily trainer with GuideRails support (no medial post). Heel strike oriented, 12mm brand drop. Outsole durability rated good (0.67mm wear); toebox durability poor at 1/5 due to thin engineered mesh/3D Fit Print upper. Midsole softness 19.5 HA. Forefoot felt thin for forefoot strikers.

Moderate stability shoe using midsole walls, sole flares and rocker instead of medial post. PWRRUN+ sockliner. Forefoot energy return 63.5%. Heel SA 127, forefoot SA 122. Midsole softness 22.3 HA. Forefoot midsole width 121.0mm, heel 104.6mm. True to size. Brand drop spec 6mm vs measured 7mm. Flexibility 10.0N.

Mild stability daily trainer using Hoka J-Frame technology instead of a traditional medial post. Firm CMEVA midsole (26.6 HA durometer). Brand drop stated 5mm vs lab 6.3mm. Narrow toebox (70.0mm); Wide version available in select regions. Forefoot energy return 56.1%, forefoot shock absorption greater than heel. Flexibility 17.8N. Outsole durability rating not fully stated in text.

Full-length fiberglass EnergyRods (not a plate). Midsole softness 38.3 AC (Lightstrike 2.0), secondary foam 29.2 AC (Lightstrike Pro). Shock absorption 128 SA. Continental rubber outsole. Toebox durability rated 1/5 (poor upper durability). Fits slightly large. Forefoot landing platform 118.2mm, heel 86.0mm. Designed for midfoot/forefoot strikers.

Super trainer with carbon plate; heel stack 42.5mm exceeds World Athletics 40mm road race limit. ASICS advertises 8mm drop but lab measured 9.6mm. Forefoot energy return 61.8%; midsole softness 31.7 AC (FF Blast+), secondary FF Turbo 33.9 AC. Stiffness 26.5N. Narrow fit (93.6mm), toebox width 74.3mm. Flexible heel counter and poor stability — neutral runners only. Lab weight 237g.

Tempo-oriented carbon-plated trainer; narrow fit and toebox (91.5mm width, 69.7mm toebox). Midsole softness 37.7 AC (Nitro) / 37.1 AC (Nitro Elite). Shock absorption 132 SA. Flexibility 20.6N. Fits slightly small. Outsole durability rated Decent. Not recommended for stability needs. Heel/forefoot midsole widths 82.7mm/109.3mm.

ZoomX = Pebax (ZOTEK PEBA by Zotefoams). Carbon Flyplate sandwiched between dual-density ZoomX (lower 34.5 AC softer, upper 47.2 AC firmer). Air Pods in forefoot. Lab drop 8.5mm (brand 8.0mm). Forefoot energy return 73.9%, heel 74.9%. Shock absorption 144 SA heel/113 SA forefoot. Outsole and toebox durability rated 'Bad'; heel padding 'Good'. Torsional rigidity 'Stiff'; heel counter stiffness 'Flexible'; traction 'Moderate'. Breathability described as best-in-class. Not orthotic friendly, non-removable insole. Strike pattern heel. Slightly small fit, medium width/toebox. Worn by Kelvin Kiptum for marathon WR.

Full-length Energyrods (carbon-infused) act as the plate. Lab drop 8.0mm vs brand-stated 6.5mm; lab forefoot 29.8mm vs brand 33mm. TPEE foam, firmer feel. Shock absorption 136 SA, midsole softness 37.6 AC, stiffness 25.3N. Continental outsole. Toebox durability rated 1/5, but outsole durability rated good. Most stable super shoe per reviewer; not suited for heel strikers. Risk of heel slippage.

Dual-midsole: PWRRUN HG top (33.5 AC) over PWRRUN PB bottom (35.1 AC) with S-shaped carbon plate. Brand lists 8mm drop; lab measured 9.5mm. Stiffness 25.7N (stiff), torsional rigidity rated 'Stiff', heel counter 'Flexible'. Heel SA 139, forefoot SA 103. Best for heel strikers; heavier than v3 (7.3oz). True to size. Stack just under 240g+35mm threshold for racer but qualifies.

Marathon super shoe. Lab heel stack 33.5mm, forefoot 30.8mm, lab drop 2.7mm (brand says 39/34mm, 5mm drop). Forefoot energy return 74.4%. Carbon plate moved higher for snappier toe-off, makes it stiffer (25.3N). Thin 2.0mm outsole, criticized for durability. Heel padding durability rated 5. Page shows conflicting price $250 in text and $500/$250 in tables. True to size, narrow fit.

Fits half size small; narrow toebox. Stiff (80.7N bend test, older methodology). Midsole softness 17.4 HA, outsole hardness 81.6 HC. Toebox durability 1/5, heel padding durability 5/5, outsole durability good (0.5mm indentation). Hoka lists drop as 5mm vs lab 6.6mm. Heel SA 141, forefoot SA 112.

Lab-measured values used for weight (277g M9), drop (3.8mm lab vs 4mm brand), and stacks (insole included). Brand claims heel 33mm/forefoot 29mm. Energy return 53.3% heel / 56.8% forefoot. Vibram outsole, neutral arch, late-stage Meta-Rocker. Narrow fit, not for wide feet. Estimated ~400 mile durability. Price shown as $320 in one spec block but comparison table and brand listing indicate $155 MSRP.

Contragrip outsole, QuickLace system. Lab drop 14.1mm but feels like 8-10mm. Firm midsole (52.9 AC durometer). Designed for muddy/soft ground and cold winter conditions; poor breathability. Toebox durability 4/5, heel padding durability 2/5, outsole durability showed 1.2mm indentation. Outsole hardness 93.9 HC. True to size. No rock plate. Lightest Speedcross yet.

Rock plate in forefoot (material unspecified), plus Trail Adapt system. Lab drop 9.2mm vs brand-claimed 8mm. TrailTack Green rubber, outsole hardness 81.8 HC, outsole wear 0.8mm (good). Toebox durability 2/5, heel padding durability 1/5. Midsole firmness 25.5 HA, firms up 32% in cold. Over 50% recycled upper. Heavier than v16 by 19g. Toebox width wide. Insole removable, orthotic friendly. Not for heavy runners per chart.

Rock plate (material not specified, likely TPU/non-carbon). Lab drop 2.2mm vs brand 4.0mm. Midsole softness 26.0 HA; PWRRUN+ plush insole. PWRTRAC outsole, hardness 92.4 HC. Energy return forefoot 64.5%. 2E width available in select markets. Not for ultras; somewhat firm ride.
